edges.description: Orphanet records hypertelorism as a very frequent craniofacial phenotype of RUNX2-related cleidocranial dysplasia.

nodes.description: Dental enamel abnormality expands the modeled CCD dental phenotype beyond eruption and tooth-number abnormalities.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000682 | Abnormality of dental enamel | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Carious teeth are a frequent dental complication in the cleidocranial dysplasia dental phenotype.
Chronic otitis media is a frequent ear manifestation reported in cleidocranial dysplasia.
Down-sloping shoulders are a common shoulder-girdle manifestation in cleidocranial dysplasia.
Hypertelorism is part of the recurrent craniofacial presentation in cleidocranial dysplasia.
Micrognathia is a frequent craniofacial skeletal manifestation of cleidocranial dysplasia.
Narrow chest reflects thoracic skeletal involvement in cleidocranial dysplasia.

RUNX2 (CBFA1) haploinsufficiency impairs osteoblast differentiation, causing defective ossification with prominent intramembranous skeletal involvement (clavicles/skull) and additional endochondral effects.
nodes.id: RUNX2 Haploinsufficiency and Osteoblast Differentiation Failure

evidence.reference_title: Cleidocranial dysplasia: clinical and molecular genetics.
evidence.snippet: Loss of both alleles (-/-) leads to a complete absence of bone owing to a lack of osteoblast differentiation.

evidence.reference_title: Cleidocranial dysplasia: clinical and molecular genetics.
evidence.snippet: CBFA1 controls differentiation of precursor cells into osteoblasts and is thus essential for membranous as well as endochondral bone formation.

molecular_functions: DNA-binding transcription factor activity, RNA polymerase II-specific

evidence.reference_title: Cleidocranial dysplasia: case report of three siblings.
evidence.snippet: is a rare autosomal dominant skeletal dysplasia caused by CBAF1 gene

evidence.reference_title: Cleidocranial dysplasia: clinical and molecular genetics.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in the CBFA1 gene that presumably lead to synthesis of an inactive gene product were identified in patients with CCD.

nodes.description: Recurrent respiratory infections are reported as a common clinical manifestation in cleidocranial dysplasia.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002205 | Recurrent respiratory infections | Very frequent (99-80%)
